NZ_MEDICAL_EXAM_INFO.title: New Zealand Visa: Medical Examination (Philippines)
description: Required for all Filipino students staying for more than 6 months, the medical process for a New Zealand student visa is similar to Australia's but uses different terminology and specific clinics.
**The "Upfront Medical" Option**
Students are highly encouraged to complete their medical exam before lodging the visa application to avoid "Stop-Start" processing delays.

list: **How it works:** The student does not need a referral letter from Immigration NZ. Simply book an "Upfront" appointment at a panel clinic.
**NZER Number:** After the exam, the clinic will give the student an eMedical Information Sheet containing a unique NZER number.
**Integration:** The student enters this NZER number directly into their RealMe/ADEPT online application, allowing the system to link their health results automatically.

[red blinking warning icon] **Critical Identity Requirement:** The Name, Passport Number, and Birthdate on the student's eMedical record must match their passport exactly. If there is a typo, the system cannot link the results, and the student may be required to redo the exam at their own expense.

description: **Authorized Panel Clinics**
The student must use a clinic approved by Immigration New Zealand. Results from regular hospitals or family doctors will not be accepted.
**What to bring?**

list: **Original Passport:** Physical passport is required; photocopies or digital copies are not accepted.

**Medical History:** If the student has a history of TB, heart conditions, or major surgeries, they must bring the official Medical Certificate/Reports and previous X-rays (if less than 6 months old).
**Vaccination Records:** Bring COVID-19 and childhood immunization records if available.
**Eyeglasses/Contacts:** Bring these if used for the vision test.

description: **Pro-Tips for Filipino Students**

list: **Menstrual Cycle:** Female applicants must not schedule their medical during their period. Clinics require a wait of at least 5â€“7 days after the period ends before performing the urinalysis.
**No Fasting:** Unlike some local check-ups, fasting is not required for NZ immigration medicals. The student should eat a light meal before the appointment.
**Photos:** Most 2025 panel clinics take a digital photo on-site for the eMedical system, but it is always safer to bring 2â€“3 recent passport-sized photos with a white background just in case.

NZ_VISA_PROCESSING_TIME.title: New Zealand Student Visa: Processing Times
description: For more information about current wait times, please visit the [official Immigration New Zealand Student Visa Wait Times page](https://www.immigration.govt.nz/study/for-education-providers/data-and-processing-times-for-international-student-visas/student-visa-wait-times/).

NZ_APPROVAL_IN_PRINCIPLE.title: New Zealand Visa: Approval in Principle (AIP)
description: This is the **'Conditional Approval'** stage. It means Immigration New Zealand (INZ) has verified the student's documents, health, and **'Bona Fide'** status and is ready to grant the visa once final financial steps are completed.
**What is AIP?**
In the Philippines, the AIP process is used to protect the studentâ€™s funds. The student does not need to pay the large tuition fee until after INZ confirms they intend to approve the visa.
**How it Works (The 2-Step Approval)**

list: **AIP Letter Received:** The student receives an email from INZ stating their application is 'Approved in Principle.'
**Fulfilling Conditions:** The letter will list 1 or 2 final requirements (usually tuition payment) that must be met within a specific timeframe (typically 10â€“15 working days).

description: **The Final Requirements**
Once the student has the AIP letter, they must complete the following to get their actual e-Visa:

list: **Tuition Fee Payment:** The student must pay the first year (or first program) of tuition fees to their NZ school.
**Evidence:** Provide the Official Receipt issued by the NZ institution.

**Funds Transfer Scheme (FTS):** If requested in the AIP letter, the student may need to open an ANZ Bank account and transfer their living expenses ($20,000 NZD) into it. This is common for students whose financial backgrounds require extra verification.
**Unconditional Offer:** If the initial offer was 'Conditional' (e.g., waiting for final grades), the student must now provide the final Unconditional Offer of Place.

description: **Important Deadlines**

list: **Payment Window:** The student usually has a very strict window to send the receipts back to INZ. If this deadline is missed, the AIP may expire, and the student might have to start the application over.
**Currency Fluctuations:** Since tuition is paid after AIP, be aware that the PHP to NZD exchange rate may have shifted since the application was first made.

NEW_ZEALAND_OFFER_OF_PLACE_INFO.title: Received the Student's Offer of Place
description: Once the Offer of Place is received, please double-check all of the student's information to ensure it is correct.
**Accuracy Check:** If all details (name, date of birth, course dates) are accurate, we can proceed with the student visa application immediately.

**Conditional Offers:** The student can now apply for their visa with a Conditional Offer of Place. Immigration New Zealand can start assessing the application and may issue an "Approval in Principle" (AIP) while the student waits for final results.
**Corrections:** If any errors are found, the student must notify their assigned consultant immediately.
**Timeline:** Receiving an updated Offer of Place typically takes an additional 3â€“5 business days.
**Note:** While the student can apply with a conditional offer, they must provide the final Unconditional Offer of Place before the visa can be fully granted.

NZ_VISA_LODGEMENT_INFO.title: New Zealand Visa Lodgement: Philippines
description: For Filipino students applying for the Fee-Paying Student Visa or Pathway Student Visa.
**The Pre-Lodgement Essentials**
Our team will help the student lodge their information. In 2025, there are two ways to lodge, but one is now highly restricted.
**Online Lodgement (The "ADEPT" Enhanced System)**

list: **RealMe Login:** The student must first create a [RealMe account](https://login.realme.govt.nz/32179062-92f6-4eb0-89bc-df400a9e0367/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?p=B2C_1A_DIA_RealMe_Home&client_id=5e90bca8-7dd9-4399-8863-340a4c002ce7&redirect_uri=https://api.realme.govt.nz/sls/continue&scope=openid&state=home&response_type=code&prompt=login). This is the secure portal used to access the application.

**ADEPT System:** Once logged in via RealMe, the student will be automatically directed to the ADEPT (Enhanced Immigration Online) platform. This is a "smart form" that customizes questions based on the student's specific situation.
**Upfront Medical:** We must enter the student's NZER number in the ADEPT form so the system can instantly verify their health results.

description: **Paper-Based Lodgement (Restricted/Phased Out)**

list: **2025 Rule:** As of September 18, 2025, paper applications for Fee-Paying and Pathway visas are no longer accepted for standard cases.
**Exceptions:** Paper forms (INZ 1012) are now only used for specific categories (like some Dependent Child visas) or if the student has a formal waiver from INZ.
**VFS Process:** If a paper application is allowed, it must be physically submitted to VFS Global in Makati or Cebu, which takes much longer to process than the digital ADEPT system.

description: Please prepare the Statement of Purpose (SOP), which must demonstrate the student is a "Bona Fide" student:

list: **Current Circumstances:** Details about their family, community, and employment ties in the Philippines. This proves they have a strong "compelling reason" to return home.
**Why choose New Zealand?** An explanation of why NZ was chosen over other destinations, and why the course isn't being taken in the Philippines.
**Course & Provider Choice:** Why this specific course and institution? Students must show they researched the NZQA Level and learning outcomes.
**Value to Future Career:** How will this NZ qualification directly increase their salary or employability in the Philippines? Be specific about target job titles.
**Study & Work History:** A logical explanation for any "career pivots" (e.g., from Nursing to IT) or any gaps in their study history.
**Other Relevant Info:** A "catch-all" section for travel history and evidence of previous visa compliance.

description: **Financial Evidence**

list: **Verification:** Bank statements from the last 6 months are required to show the "source of wealth."
**Note:** Large "lump sum" deposits without a clear explanation (like a property sale or bank loan) are frequently flagged by the ADEPT system's risk algorithms.

description: [DEPENDENTS_INFO]
**Payment Method**

list: **Online (RealMe/ADEPT):** Paid via Credit or Debit Card (Visa/Mastercard) directly in the portal.
**VFS Fee:** A separate â‚±1,100 PHP fee is paid at VFS for passport handling/scanning services.

NZ_WORK_RIGHTS_INFO.title: NZ Student Visa Work Rights (2025/2026)
description: For students studying at Tertiary level (Degrees, Diplomas) or Secondary School (Years 12 & 13).
**The "25-Hour Weekly" Rule**
Definition of a Week: Any 7-day period (usually Monday to Sunday).
**While Course is in Session:** As of November 3, 2025, eligible students can work a maximum of 25 hours per week.
**Important Note:** Unlike Australia, you cannot "average" hours over two weeks. If you work 30 hours one week and 20 the next, you have breached your visa.

**Practical Work:** If your course requires "Work Placement" or "Practicum" hours (e.g., Nursing or Teaching students), these hours are not part of your 25-hour limit, provided they are a mandatory requirement of your qualification.
**Holiday Periods**
**Unlimited Work:** You can work unlimited hours during official scheduled breaks (e.g., Summer/Christmas holidays), provided your course is at least 1 academic year long and worth 120 credits.
**Masters & PhD:** Students enrolled in a Masters by research or Doctoral (PhD) program have unlimited work rights all year round.
**Pay & Minimum Wage (2025/2026)**
**Adult Minimum Wage:** $23.50 NZD per hour (effective April 1, 2025).
**Starting-Out/Training Wage:** $18.80 NZD per hour (applies to some 16-19 year olds).
**Key Conditions**
**Variation of Conditions (VOC):** If your current visa still says "20 hours," you must apply for a VOC and pay the $325 NZD fee before you can legally work the new 25-hour limit.
**No Working Early:** You cannot begin working until your course has actually started.
**IRD Number Required:** You must apply for an Inland Revenue (IRD) number to ensure you are taxed correctly.
**Prohibited Work:** You are strictly forbidden from working in the adult entertainment industry (e.g., sex work).
